title: Galanz GLR10TWEEFR True Top Freezer Retro Refrigerator Frost Free Dual Door Fridge, Adjustable Electrical Thermostat Control, 10.0 Cu Ft, White

cooling_failure:
Do not buy - has already stopped cooling 3 times, less than a year and a half since purchase. It's unreliable, as it will randomly just stop cooling (the number inside, the coolness setting, will start blinking first then it just drops cooling) and I have to move everything out while we try to figure out what's going on. I really just wish I hadn't bought this fridge.

Don’t be lured in to buying these… Adorable refrigerators that last about 3 months. Quit working within a year.  Have an extended warranty but they won't let me use it because they state it's under manfacure warranty.  Amazon is no help, useless, states that return window has closed.  Don't buy anything from these people

Literally 2 months later, we came to work after the weeke

The Galanz service was nonexistent. The refrigerator was delivered on time but did not work when we plugged it in. The office says they spent two weeks looking for someone to come service the unit. Their answer was to offer a refund…. If we disabled the unit, submitted excessive documentation, waited 8-10 weeks for a refund and then disposed of the refrigerator ourselves. Worthless warranty! I bought a retro frig july 2020 , the temp control has never worked correct , it freezes everything even on lowest setting, then died and wont even turn on, but what is worse is the so called warranty, i spent an entire week of trying to get them to respond to us to get repairs  to no avail. control_failure: Had it for a month and have tested it with a thermometer since the day I got it. I don't open it a lot since I recently moved, but it is my only fridge. There are no problems with the freezer so far, I love the look, everything appears sealed correctly, the doors work fine, and it's level. I cannot get it between 35-38 degrees F, though. It lasted only one night. I had to wait for over two months for the manufacturer to order a control board, but they didn’t come-very bad product.
physical_breakage: The fridge arrived late…the handles are plastic and were broken. Working the phone for a month trying to get them to just send a replacement handle. Honestly, this is the worst customer service I’ve ever experienced. The product is made in China…I wouldn’t buy a Galanz product again…they are awful.

There's a lot of plastic on it. I've had it for 6 months now and the inner plastic shelves on the door are breaking, the white plastic liner on the shelves are broken and have fallen off. The freezer never really sealed well and it freezes over. I keep a spatula in the door to knock the ice down. It is super cute.

[[VIDEOID:4b22cefeb553c2159f4dcb00e4b8d9cd]] It was cute and affordable but faulty handles that failed in less than a month. Plastic inserts to attach handles.  Hoping the 6 year warranty works in our favor. Watching for  a response from the seller.
leak: My 5 year old Frigidaire decided to start leaking, which would cost over $600 to repair. I paid $400 for it at a black Friday sale 5 years ago. Thanks to inflation, comparable sized models now cost $700 or more on back Friday sale. If new appliances are just going to break in 5 years anyway, I couldn't justify spending $700 or more. So, this was the largest fridge I could find that was on sale for
motor_failure: This refrigerator broke down almost a year to the purchase date. Also, there was a section in the top shelf of the fridge that froze and ruined food. The "freezing part" happened almost immediately, but I didn't want to deal with the hassle of returning a refrigerator. I wish I did. Very cute, but a lemon.
